category.name: Form Fields
category.preferred_workflow: Use Aspose.Words DocumentBuilder form field APIs and FormField collection directly. Create local sample documents when required and operate on real form field nodes.

strict_api_bans: Do not assume form fields already exist in a document.
Do not mix StructuredDocumentTag unless the task explicitly requires content controls.
Do not invent unsupported form-field helper APIs.
Do not skip validation of field existence when the task expects an existing field.
canonical_patterns: Use DocumentBuilder.InsertTextInput, InsertCheckBox, and InsertComboBox to create legacy form fields.
Use FormField.Result for text-based values and FormField.Checked for checkboxes.
Bootstrap local source documents whenever the task implies an existing file or pre-existing fields.
nullable_warning_prevention: Check for null before accessing form fields or lookup results.
Avoid CS8600, CS8602, and CS8604 by guarding values before dereference.
Do not rely on maybe-null field lookups without validation.

common_failure_patterns.pattern: Mixing legacy form fields with content controls
common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples use StructuredDocumentTag when the task is about legacy form fields.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use DocumentBuilder legacy form-field APIs and Document.Range.FormFields unless the task explicitly requires content controls.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Code assumes unsupported helpers or reads the wrong property for the field type.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Access legacy fields through Range.FormFields and use Result, Checked, or DropDownItems according to the field type.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Examples assume an existing document with form fields already exists.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create a local sample document and seed the required form fields before reading or updating them.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example saves output but does not verify that the form field exists or that its value changed.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate field existence and the final assigned value before finishing.

examples.task: Insert a text input form field with placeholder text using DocumentBuilder in a new document.
Add a check box form field, set default checked state and custom size via DocumentBuilder.
Create a combo box form field containing three items and set default selected index using DocumentBuilder.
Specify a name when inserting a text input field to automatically generate a matching bookmark.
Insert a text input field with maximum length of 50 characters and numeric format using DocumentBuilder.
Insert a text input field with custom date format and default current date value using DocumentBuilder.
Implement a reusable method that adds a combo box form field with customizable items and default index.
Assign a unique name to each inserted form field to ensure distinct automatic bookmarks.
Batch insert multiple text input form fields into a template using a loop over field definitions.
Load a DOCX file, change combo box selections programmatically, and save the modified document.
Update result values of several check box form fields based on external JSON configuration data.
Set the Result property of a text input form field to a predefined string value.
Read the Result property of a check box form field to determine whether it is checked.
Toggle the checked state of a specific check box form field based on external configuration settings.
Change the size of an existing check box form field programmatically to improve visual consistency.
Retrieve a form field by its name from a loaded document and read its Result property.
Access a form field by index from the FormFields collection and modify its Result value.
Use FormField.Type enumeration to differentiate between text input, check box, and combo box fields.
Iterate over all form fields in a document and list each field's name and type.
Count the number of each form field type by iterating through the FormFields collection.
Iterate through form fields and log each field's Result value for debugging purposes.
Extract automatically generated bookmark names for all form fields and store them in a lookup dictionary.
Navigate to a form field using its automatically created bookmark and extract surrounding paragraph text.
Protect a Word document with AllowOnlyFormFields option, allowing only form fields to be edited.
Apply document protection levels ensuring only form fields remain editable while other sections are read‑only.
Verify that protected document still permits editing of form fields after saving and reopening.
After inserting fields, protect the document and then verify that non‑field content cannot be edited.
Disable all check box form fields programmatically when a specific document condition is met.
Implement error handling for attempts to insert a form field with an empty name, logging a warning.
Build a console application that reads a CSV file and populates corresponding text input fields in a template.
